elements.title: a Minnesota church building
elements.contents: ... that a Minnesota church building (pictured) is considered to be a "historic non-operating cemetery" because a former priest was buried in a crypt underneath the sanctuary?

elements.title: Apollon Karelin
elements.contents: ... that Apollon Karelin established an order of the Knights Templar to promote anarchism in Soviet Russia?

elements.title: an Abbott Elementary episode
elements.contents: ... that an Abbott Elementary episode was filmed at an abandoned shopping mall, after which it was demolished to build an NFL facility?

elements.title: Michael Lippman
elements.contents: ... that Michael Lippman had his breakthrough in the music business in the 1970s by working as an attorney and manager for David Bowie?

elements.title: Complications in Sue
elements.contents: ... that the opera Complications in Sue was written by ten different composers, each writing without knowledge of what the others were doing?

elements.title: Kerri Rawson
elements.contents: ... that Kerri Rawson criticized author Stephen King writing a novella based on her serial killer father's crimes?

elements.title: The Witcher: The Adventure Card Game
elements.contents: ... that one reviewer said The Witcher: The Adventure Card Game was "a very pretty piece of junk"?

elements.title: Lorong Chuan station
elements.contents: ... that the public art at Lorong Chuan station depicts landmarks, the Merlion, and political events like the Pedra Branca dispute?

elements.title: Brad
elements.contents: ... that NFL players Brad and Cory Lekkerkerker worked on a farm with 2,000 cows and were nicknamed the "Brothers Large"?
